V. Good explanation of the common understanding but it is just not _right...._
 The Heisenberg uncertainty principal in not based on physical measurement it is innate to nature.

author: @cryptonein
text: While you do explain this succinctly, it needs to be noted that legacy physics such as this is not the only offering of explanation. Physics has been manipulated in very evil ways since roughly 1890 and this is very demonstrable with history and actual facts and data. The current physics that is largely taught and accepted by academia is a government needed insert to facilitate blocks from discovering the deeper nature of the universe and to prevent more accurate modeling. Work from Ionel Dinu, Lori Gardi, Robert Distinti, Dayton Miller, and thousands of others (there is a fantastic German book that enumerates essentially 100 years of experiments that disprove the foundation of current legacy physics) have offered both empirical evidence and alternative modeling that are opening our understanding as to not only new options, but also what happened to society that got us to this conundrum. Please read the book from the late (recently deceased) Dr. James DeMeo: "The Dynamic Ether of Cosmic Space: Correcting a Major Error in Modern Science" for a fantastic treatment of exceptionally well documented history (you can ignore the other elements of the book if you wish). You will be very pleased with the time you invest in reading this book.

author: @peterclark6290
text: IOW mankind will never be able to peer directly into the machinery of an atom. But we can start with the knowledge is that the Cosmos and everything in it works; according to a binding set of rules. Which any engineer and designer can tell you has to be simple, perhaps as simple as a binary logic based on energy states being attractive or negative and how they interact in wave form. So unless the perpetual-energy sub-sub-atomic particles have actual mass, everything is just energy. Thus the Laws Physics are seeking are in turn merely compounded binary logic, requiring merely a handful of constants (maximum speed, wave pathways and patterns). There is no uncertainty: just a permanent barrier. Any uncertainty is the vain hope that we will overcome the constants and take a photo of a photon, or its constituent parts. We have until Entropy deconstructs the whole kit and kaboodle to waste our brightest minds looking backward. As always it is 'what you do with it' that counts.

author: @PetraKann
text:
Well explained.
A photon doesn’t actually “bounce” off an electron during a collision. 
When an electron is hit by a photon of light, it absorbs the quanta of energy the photon was carrying and moves to a higher energy state. One way of thinking about this higher energy state is to imagine that the electron is now moving faster, (it has just been "hit" by a rapidly moving photon).
There are other Conjugate variable pairs apart from position and momentum - for example Energy and Time (ΔΕΔt>=h/2),
Some physicists claim you can have more than two variables coupled in this way via a Heisenberg type uncertainty principle.

author: @totherarf
text:
So to paraphrase : ..... 
..... The amount of sense Rob (or me) makes is proportional to the amount of beer consumed until someone tries to understand what is meant wherein the waveform collapses and the true chaotic nature of reality is glimpsed! 

Or to put in Northern parlance The Heisenberg's Beer Goggles work on your ears too!

replies.author: @matthewellisor5835
replies.text:
Always a jab, isn't it, at the folks from the 'North' and at those who's antecedents emigrated from those lands? 

I submit, for your consideration, a challenge: To debate on the topic of the philosophic interpretation of quantum mechanics, conducted in the form of a drinking contest. 

I would select the beverages and the questions/positions, you would, of course, select the venue, moderator and who would argue each position. I'm happy to travel up to 200km provided that childcare is available.

I'm only mostly kidding.

replies.author: @totherarf
replies.text:
@matthewellisor5835 Hmmm ........ You know I hail from that bastion of the North? In Salford!
I see through your cunning plan though and it has backfired on you!

Firstly I am not a child despite having many of the attributes of one and so childcare is superfluous ;o)
Secondly if you chose both beverage and question the only variable allowed so as not to collapse the wave function would be to drink or not to drink! That duality would allow a fair competition whilst at the same time allowing me to chose weather or not to drink said beverage and weather you get to drink yours!
This would be a challenge few northerners could refuse!

As a Northerner, you would never truly be sure if I were joking.

Yes I am! ;o)

But to quote a southern jessie ....... There are more things on Earth than dreamed of in all your philosophies Horatio! 
On a rare serious note though, I would like to hear your philosophic interpretation! 
Every day is a good day if you learn something!

replies.author: @claudesully
replies.text: Got my popcorn 😁 Please don't stop, this is actually better than Netflix🤣

replies.author: @matthewellisor5835
replies.text:
@totherarf The probabilistic nature of your apparent attempt at humour notwithstanding, I believe that you misconstrue my meaning and the nomenclature attributed to me by my neighbors. 'roun' these parts, I would likely be called a "Southerner" Or, more likely, some more disparaging term; no matter that my forefathers were Lords of many sad little hills before their voyage across the big water.

To the matter (yes, I preempted that pun) at cat, we are between conventional materialistic ontologies (that which would characterize nature as fundamentally atomic; consisting of deterministic finite bits of matter with inertia and interactions which are continuous through space and time.) any that which characterizes the universe as a collection of finite bits whose notions and interactions, when measured, appear to be probabilistic or discontinuous rather than fluid.

Does an election, excited or relaxing, make an "instantaneous leap" between energy levels? Or, does the discrete difference in location require that the "universal speed limit" be preserved, And, so, where in that temporal quantum, does the electron reside without either "allowable" energy? 

Please pardon any delay in response, it is time for me to attend to childcare of children.

I hold the opinion that it's more like: 
"We don't know precisely just how much we don't know about a particular topic; And, if we were to know precisely how much we did not know, we wouldn't know anything at all about just _what_ _it_ _was_ that we knew, that we did not know, a particular amount about."

There, clear as mud? ;D
